segments.title: A fallen Commonwealth and one ship's mission
segments.summary: The series introduction makes Andromeda and her crew the agents charged with restoring civilization after the Systems Commonwealth falls.
segments.body: The opening presents the Systems Commonwealth as a great civilization destroyed by a long night. Against that collapse, one starship and one crew vow to restore light and civilization. The premise gives Andromeda a mission larger than winning battles: the ship becomes a surviving institution around which political order might be rebuilt. This brief watch point establishes the fictional stakes before Dr. Clarke turns to the vessel's cruiser identity.

evidence.note: The introduction describes the Commonwealth's fall and Andromeda's vow to rekindle civilization.

segments.title: Andromeda's cruiser spirit
segments.summary: The introduction frames the starship Andromeda as a cruiser whose confidence meets an even larger, stronger opponent.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke treats Andromeda's identity as a cruiser as the key to the scene's humour and character. Captain Dylan Hunt asks whether the ship is frightened, and Andromeda answers that she is terrified by an opponent larger and stronger than herself. Her surprise that the familiar warning could apply to her captures the confidence of a powerful cruiser that is accustomed to overmatching lesser vessels. The exchange uses a fictional starship to express a recognizable naval type: capable and self-assured, but not the largest combatant in every fight.

evidence.note: Dr. Clarke identifies Andromeda as a cruiser and explains why its reaction to a larger opponent fits that role.
